name: SQLite – Blog: list users with posts
description: List the first 5 users from the local SQLite blog database, each with their posts embedded.

name: SQLite – Blog: users → posts → comments (3 levels)
description: 3-level traversal: list the first 3 users from the local SQLite blog database, each with their posts, and each post with its comments. Demonstrates cross-entity deep nesting within a single database.

name: SQLite – Blog: introspect schema
description: Inspect the tables and columns of the local SQLite blog database.

name: SQLite – Chinook: artists → albums → tracks (3 levels)
description: 3-level traversal: list the first 3 artists from the Chinook music-store database, each with their albums, and each album with its tracks. Requires chinook.db in the working directory.

name: PostgreSQL – RNAcentral: list RNA sequences
description: List 10 RNA sequences from the public RNAcentral PostgreSQL database at EBI. Uses the publicly documented read-only credentials from https://rnacentral.org/help/public-database

name: PostgreSQL – RNAcentral: sequences → xrefs → source databases (3 levels)
description: 3-level cross-entity traversal: list 3 RNA sequences, each with their cross-references, and each cross-reference resolved to its source database record. Demonstrates a 3-hop graph walk within the RNAcentral PostgreSQL database.

name: Neo4j – Movies demo: actors and movies
description: Query the public Neo4j Movies demo database for the first 5 Movie nodes. Uses the publicly documented read-only credentials from https://demo.neo4jlabs.com

name: Neo4j – Movies demo: people with movies acted in
description: Query the Neo4j Movies demo for the first 5 Person nodes, each with the movies they acted in via the ACTED_IN relationship.

name: REST – JSONPlaceholder: posts with comments
description: Fetch the first 5 posts from JSONPlaceholder and embed their comments.

name: REST – Art Institute of Chicago: artworks with artist
description: Fetch 5 artworks from the Art Institute of Chicago public API, each with its artist record embedded. Uses the `fields` API parameter via `where` to limit response payload.

name: REST – SpaceX: launches with rocket and launchpad
description: Fetch the last 5 SpaceX launches, each with its rocket and launchpad details resolved via belongsTo relations. The `rocket` and `launchpad` fields on each launch are IDs that are resolved by fetching /rockets/{id} and /launchpads/{id}.

name: REST – SpaceX: payloads → launch → rocket (3 levels, cross-entity REST)
description: 3-level cross-entity traversal entirely within the SpaceX REST API: list 3 payloads, resolve each payload's parent launch, and then resolve each launch's rocket. Demonstrates a 3-hop belongsTo chain across 3 entity types in a single REST database.

name: REST – Fake Store: carts with user details
description: Fetch 5 shopping carts from the Fake Store API, each with its owner resolved via a belongsTo relation on the userId field.

name: REST – Rick and Morty: first 10 characters
description: List the first 10 characters from the Rick and Morty API. Results are automatically unwrapped from the {results:[...]} envelope.

name: REST – SWAPI: Star Wars planets
description: List the first 10 Star Wars planets from SWAPI. Results are automatically unwrapped from the {results:[...]} envelope.

name: REST – Studio Ghibli: films
description: List all Studio Ghibli films from the public Ghibli API.

name: REST – CoinGecko: top 10 coins by market cap
description: List the top 10 cryptocurrencies ranked by market cap from the CoinGecko free API. The vs_currency param is required.

name: REST – NASA: Astronomy Picture of the Day
description: Fetch today's NASA Astronomy Picture of the Day. Use DEMO_KEY for testing (30 req/hour), or register for a free key at api.nasa.gov.

name: REST – GitHub: search popular TypeScript repos
description: Search GitHub for the most-starred TypeScript repositories using the public search API. No auth required for read-only queries.

name: REST – Open Trivia: 10 science questions
description: Fetch 10 multiple-choice science questions from the Open Trivia Database free API.

name: REST – MusicBrainz: search for artists
description: Search MusicBrainz for artists matching a query. Rate limit is 1 req/sec; the User-Agent header is required. Pass your search term via `where: { query: "artist name" }`.

name: REST – Open Brewery DB: micro-breweries in the US
description: List 10 micro-breweries in the United States from the Open Brewery DB free public API.

name: REST – RestCountries: European countries
description: List the first 10 European countries with name, capital, population and flags.

name: REST – Open-Meteo: current weather in Berlin
description: Get the current weather forecast for Berlin from the Open-Meteo free API.

name: REST – PokéAPI: first 10 Pokémon
description: List the first 10 Pokémon from the PokéAPI.

name: REST – Jikan: top anime
description: List the top 5 anime titles from the Jikan MyAnimeList API.

name: Catalog – list all available databases
description: Return all pre-defined catalog entries with their entity names, columns, and relationships. Shows the breadth of data sources queryable through one /graphql endpoint.

name: MySQL – Rfam: top RNA families by species count
description: Query the public Rfam MySQL database at EBI for the 10 RNA families annotated in the most species. Demonstrates the MySQL driver against a real bioinformatics database. Uses the publicly documented read-only credentials from https://rfam.org/help/database.

name: MySQL – Rfam: families with sequence regions (2 levels)
description: List 5 Rfam RNA families with their highest-scoring full-region sequence hits embedded. Demonstrates a hasMany join across the family and full_region tables in the Rfam MySQL database.

name: OWL ingestion – Blog ontology (Turtle)
description: Parse the blog OWL ontology in Turtle format and extract classes, object properties and data properties. Shows how Mozg maps an OWL TBox to a GraphQL type system.

name: OWL – generate schema (read-only, no cache)
description: Parse the blog ontology and preview the generated GraphQL SDL without caching. Useful for iterating on an ontology design before committing it. Uses generateSchema instead of ingestOntology.
